lines: Another cross, older than the one upstairs. The body on it died a long time ago and went hard and pale, no larger than any man. Its ribcage is still cracked and butterflied open the way the Choir leaves them, the wings of bone gone grey and the cavity behind them dry. The head has opened all the way into a long goat-skull, one horn curled back and one snapped off for a relic.

This was not the first saint they made here, only the first you have found gone cold. The Choir has been feeding this chapel longer than your writ admits. Whatever they are practicing for, they have had the practice.
